title: odontites vulgaris subsp. pumilus
intro: Odontites vulgaris subsp. pumilus, also known as Dwarf Toothwort, is a perennial plant belonging to the borage family (Boraginaceae) and is native to Europe. Traditionally, Dwarf Toothwort has been used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties, making it effective in treating various health issues such as respiratory problems, wounds, and digestive issues. The plant's health benefits include its ability to soothe sore throats, reduce fever, and act as a natural antiseptic. Dwarf Toothwort can be prepared as a herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, and is also used in traditional folk medicine, particularly in Eastern European and Asian cultures.
